name: Class III bistable switch: VHL–HIF oxygen sensing
description: Under normoxia, VHL degrades HIF1α; under hypoxia, HIF1α accumulates and activates VEGF, GLUT1, and glycolytic genes that positive-feedback stabilize the hypoxic state (bistability around oxygen threshold). Illustration palette matches empirical sequel topology figures—not §3.2 five-category defaults.

flowchart TD O2["Oxygen
level"]:::signal --> VHL["VHL
E3 ligase"]:::gene VHL -.->|degrades
(normoxia)| HIF["HIF1α"]:::gene HIF -->|activates
(hypoxia)| Targets["VEGF, GLUT1,
glycolytic genes"]:::outcome Targets -->|positive

benchmark_note: Companion reports mean Pearson r = 0.921 for VHL-associated Class III illustration panel (highest among Class III schematic examples shown there); aggregate Class III difficulty vs Class I assessed separately in that paper.
